question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/resources/graphql/query.graphqls -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account(input: AccountInput!)] Can an unprivileged external user use the autoRenewAccount field returned by account(input: AccountInput!) on a fee-collector account to identify the account responsible for paying auto-renewal fees, and then drain that account's balance to prevent fee-collector account renewal, causing the fee-collector to expire and disrupting fee collection?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/resources/graphql/query.graphqls -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account(input: AccountInput!)] Can an unprivileged external user query the autoRenewPeriod: Duration field on a fee-schedule account via account(input: AccountInput!) and supply an ISO 8601 duration with nanosecond precision to probe the Duration scalar parser for overflow or parsing errors that could cause incorrect auto-renewal fee calculations?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/resources/graphql/query.graphqls -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account(input: AccountInput!)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Object scalar type returned by the key field in account(input: AccountInput!) to receive an arbitrarily complex key structure that, when serialized, causes excessive memory allocation in the GraphQL response serializer, leading to a denial-of-service that prevents fee schedule reads?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/resources/graphql/query.graphqls -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account(input: AccountInput!)] Can an unprivileged external user use the id: ID field returned by account(input: AccountInput!) to obtain the opaque GraphQL node ID of a fee-collector account, and then use this ID in subsequent AccountInput.id queries to directly resolve the fee-collector account without knowing its entityId, alias, or evmAddress?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/resources/graphql/query.graphqls -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account(input: AccountInput!)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a GraphQL query with a very large number of nested autoRenewAccount.autoRenewAccount chains to cause the server to perform a depth-first traversal of the account graph, consuming excessive CPU and memory resources and degrading the availability of fee-related account queries for legitimate users?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/resources/graphql/query.graphqls -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: account(input: AccountInput!)] Can an unprivileged external user supply an AccountInput.alias with a Base32 string that decodes to exactly 20 bytes matching a known fee-collector EVM address, causing the alias lookup path to resolve the same account as the evmAddress path but bypassing evmAddress-specific validation logic and returning fee data through an unintended resolution path?
